data.title: 137 Ventures Raises Over $700 Million Across Two New Funds, Surpasses $15 Billion in AUM
data.description: A growth-stage venture firm, 137 Ventures, based in San Francisco, revealed on April 30, 2026, that they are supporting long-standing technology leaders such as Anduril, Gusto, Ramp, and SpaceX by extending their private lifecycles. This firm has successfully backed these companies, and it just sealed a deal to continue its support.

data.title: Treasury secretary reveals US seized over $500M in Iranian crypto assets, exceeding the reported $344M freeze.
data.description: The Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced that the United States has successfully confiscated approximately $500 million in cryptocurrency assets linked to Iran. This amount exceeds the previously disclosed freeze of $344 million.

data.title: Shinhan Card from South Korea partners with Solana Foundation to trial stablecoin payments and test DeFi services.
data.description: Shinhan Card has entered into an agreement with the Solana Foundation to broaden stablecoin payment trials and investigate non-custodial wallets and DeFi services. This partnership aims to explore innovative financial technologies and enhance services provided by Shinhan Card to its customers. The collaboration signifies the growing interest in digital payment solutions and the potential for decentralized finance to revolutionize traditional banking systems. The agreement highlights the commitment of both parties to push the boundaries of fintech and provide cutting-edge services. This development showcases the increasing importance of collaboration between traditional financial institutions and blockchain projects for the advancement of the industry.

data.title: Draft Australian payments plan suggests stablecoins & tokenized fiat could revolutionize money movement on future rails.
data.description: A preliminary plan for Australia's internal payment system suggests that stablecoins and tokenized fiat currency could potentially transform the way money is transferred through upcoming channels.

data.title: APi Group Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
data.description: APi Group Corporation (NYSE: APG) based in New Brighton, Minnesota, has released its financial results for the quarter ending on March 31, 2026. The company's President and CEO, Russ Becker, announced a promising beginning to the year, with a 10% increase in organic net revenue and a 70 basis points rise in adjusted EBITDA margins compared to the previous year. These positive results were driven by growth in both the Safety Services and Specialty Services segments. The company did not disclose further details regarding specific revenue or earnings figures.

data.title: PHINIA Reports First Quarter 2026 Results - Strategic Business Wins Advance Diversification Across End Markets and Alternative Fuels
data.description: PHINIA Inc., a renowned industrial supplier and global innovator in fuel systems, electrical systems, and aftermarket solutions, recently announced its first quarter results for the period ending March 31, 2026. During this quarter, the company recorded impressive net sales of $878 million, signifying a notable 10.3% rise from the previous year's first quarter. This increase in net sales equated to $39 million and $14 million when excluding the effects of foreign currency fluctuations and the SEM acquisition, respectively.

data.title: Distribution Solutions Group Announces 2026 First Quarter Results
data.description: Fort Worth, Texas-based company, Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:DSGR) announced its consolidated results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2026. DSG, a leading specialty distribution company, shared unaudited figures in a press release, with further details available in an earnings presentation on their investor website. For more information and reconciliations of GAAP to non-GAAP measures, visit https://investor.distributionsolutionsgroup.com/news/events.

data.title: Huaqin Posts Solid Q1 Growth
data.description: Huaqin Co. Ltd., an electronics manufacturer with stock codes 3296.HK and 603296.SH, announced a significant double-digit growth in the first quarter after its recent listing in Hong Kong. The company's revenue for the quarter surged by 16.4% year-on-year to 40.75 billion yuan ($6 billion), with profits also rising by 25.96% to 1.06 billion yuan. This growth was credited to the success of the company's "3+N+3" smart product platform strategy, focusing on smartphones, laptops, data center equipment, and new areas like robotics, software, and automotive electronics. Despite challenges with fluctuating material prices, Huaqin's mobile terminals business which includes smartphones, tablets, and wearables saw growth both domestically and internationally due to new product launches.

data.title: Xtract One chosen to enhance school safety for all Granville County Public Schools.
data.description: In new data released today, it has been reported that the global poverty rate has decreased significantly over the past decade. The number of people living in extreme poverty, defined as living on less than $1.90 a day, has fallen to 9.2% in 2020, a record low. This represents a decrease of around 2% since 2019. However, despite this positive trend, there are still an estimated 700 million people living in extreme poverty around the world. Sub-Saharan Africa remains the region with the highest poverty rate, with over 40% of its population living in extreme poverty. Additionally, the COVID-19 pandemic is expected to have a significant impact on poverty levels, with an estimated 120 million people being pushed into extreme poverty due to the economic fallout. This data underscores the urgent need for continued efforts to address poverty and its root causes on a global scale.

data.title: How To Earn $500 A Month From Chevron Stock Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description: The energy company Chevron Corporation (NYSE: CVX) is set to release its first-quarter earnings on May 1. Analysts project earnings of 97 cents per share, a decrease from $2.18 per share in the same period last year. The consensus forecast for Chevron's quarterly revenue is $52.7 billion, compared to $47.61 billion in the previous year. Analysts, including Paul Cheng from Scotiabank, have maintained Chevron at Sector Perform with a price target of $187, up from $168. Chevron currently offers an annual dividend yield of 3.70%, amounting to a quarterly dividend of $1.78 per share ($7.12 annually). Investors are considering leveraging the company's dividend yield for potential gains. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Vecima Announces Q3 Fiscal 2026 Results Earnings Call Thursday, May 14, 2026 at 1 pm ET
data.description: Vecima, based in Victoria, British Columbia, has disclosed plans for a conference call and live audio webcast set for Thursday, May 14, 2026, at 1pm ET. The event aims to delve into the company's performance for Q3 Fiscal 2026.
